methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $1.1B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.6B - $250.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Modal is a Series E artificial intelligence and software company founded by Erik Bernhardsson and Akshat Bubna, currently valued at $1.1B post-money on $111M in total capital raised. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe comprising 5 same-stage, same-sector private comparables (xAI, Crusoe Energy, SandboxAQ, Ayar Labs, WEKA) and 1 public software reference point (Palantir).

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach applies a 1.4x multiplier typical of Series E advancement to the $1.1B last post-money valuation, implying $1.5B. The peer-median method benchmarks Modal against the five Series E AI & Software peers, which exhibit a median valuation of $5.6B (range: $1.6B to $250B). The wide dispersion—driven by xAI's $250B valuation at the high end and WEKA's $1.6B at the low end—reflects heterogeneous investor views on AI infrastructure and software maturity within this cohort.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.3B to $6.4B, with a midpoint of $3.6B. The low end anchors to stage-progression logic and the lower quartile of peer valuations, while the high end reflects median peer-group positioning. Key uncertainties include the appropriate weight given to outliers like xAI within the peer set, whether Modal's product positioning maps to infrastructure or application-layer comparables, and near-term market conditions affecting AI software valuations.
